description: You are the senior ITSM administrator at a mid-sized company. Three open tickets — 1001 (keyboard not responding), 1003 (printer not printing), and 1004 (VPN dropping) — have been traced to a single root cause: a failing network switch in Building A. Execute the complete ITIL problem-to-resolution lifecycle. Create a Problem titled 'Faulty Network Switch IDF-A2 - Building A Connectivity Failures' with High impact and High urgency, and link service requests 1001, 1003, and 1004 as related incidents. Document the Root Cause Analysis: Root Cause is 'Cisco Catalyst 9300 in closet IDF-A2 has intermittent port failures due to firmware bug CSCvz12345 in IOS-XE 17.9.1', Symptoms are 'Wired devices on Building A Floors 1-3 lose connectivity every 2-4 hours affecting printing, VPN, and KVM-over-IP', and Workaround is 'Bounce affected ports via CLI: interface range gi1/0/1-24, shutdown, no shutdown'. Create an Emergency Change Request titled 'Emergency Firmware Upgrade - Cisco Catalyst 9300 IDF-A2' with High impact and a backout plan: 'Downgrade to IOS-XE 17.6.3 using install remove inactive'. Link this Change to the Problem. Create a Knowledge Base solution titled 'Cisco Catalyst 9300 Port Failures - Firmware Bug CSCvz12345' documenting the issue and resolution. Finally, resolve the Problem with resolution referencing the emergency change. Log in with username: administrator, password: administrator.
